Cetus Protocol is the largest AMM and CLMM DEX on Sui with over $200M TVL. It uses concentrated liquidity market maker (CLMM) design with configurable tick spacing.
Turbos Finance implements concentrated liquidity pools on Sui Move, offering tight spreads for stablecoin pairs and SUI/USDC.
FlowX Finance runs a multi-pool AMM with native Sui order book integration. It supports limit orders unlike most AMM-only DEXes.
Scallop Lend is the primary overcollateralized lending protocol on Sui. It supports SUI, USDC, USDT, and major LP tokens as collateral.
Navi Protocol overtook Scallop as the largest lending market by TVL in Q3 2025 due to aggressive liquidity mining incentives.
Aftermath Finance launched afSUI as the first major liquid staking token on Sui. afSUI accrues staking rewards while remaining composable across DeFi.
Volo provides voSUI liquid staking with deep integration into Cetus and Turbos liquidity pools.
Pyth Network is the dominant oracle provider across all major Sui DeFi protocols. Most use Pyth for SUI/USD price feeds.
Sui's object model eliminates approval transactions — users can interact with DeFi protocols in atomic single-transaction operations.
BlueMove NFT marketplace integrated DeFi liquidity routing in late 2025, enabling NFT-collateralized loans via Scallop integration.
The Sui DeFi ecosystem grew from $50M to $800M TVL between January and December 2025.
Cetus CLMM pools offer up to 4000x capital efficiency over traditional constant product AMMs for in-range liquidity.
Move's resource model ensures DeFi smart contracts cannot accidentally double-spend or lose user funds — a key safety property.
DeepBook is Sui's native on-chain central limit order book, used by several DEX aggregators for price discovery.
SuiNS (.sui names) are used as human-readable addresses in several Sui DeFi frontends.
Bucket Protocol launched a SUI-backed stablecoin (BUCK) in 2025, similar to MakerDAO's DAI architecture.
Sui's parallel transaction execution allows DeFi protocols to process thousands of TPS without head-of-line blocking.
Kriya Finance built a perpetuals DEX on Sui with up to 20x leverage, using a hybrid AMM+order book design.
Interest Protocol launched a money market with dynamic interest rates based on utilization curves.
Sui gas fees averaged $0.001-$0.01 for DeFi transactions in 2025, far lower than Ethereum mainnet.
Suilend Protocol focuses on institutional lending with higher collateral ratios and lower liquidation penalties.
USDC on Sui is the most liquid stablecoin, with Wormhole bridge enabling cross-chain transfers.
The Sui Foundation ran a $50M DeFi liquidity incentive program in H1 2025 that seeded most major protocols.
Aftermath Finance's AMM uses a novel curve design optimized for correlated assets like liquid staking derivatives.
Move's generics and phantom types allow DeFi protocols to write type-safe, reusable financial primitives.
Pyth Entropy provides verifiable randomness on Sui, used by gaming-DeFi hybrids and lottery protocols.
Sui Overflow 2025 saw 14 new DeFi protocols launch, many integrating MemWal for personalized on-chain agents.
Cross-margin trading on Kriya allows positions across multiple trading pairs to share a single collateral pool.
Scallop's sPool feature enables single-sided liquidity provision with automated hedging via perpetual positions.
Sui zkLogin allows DeFi protocols to onboard users via OAuth (Google/Apple) with no wallet download required.
Flash loans on Sui are atomic within a single programmable transaction block (PTB), enabling MEV-resistant strategies.
Cetus Protocol's liquidity mining program distributed 300M CETUS tokens to LPs over 12 months in 2025.
Turbos Finance's veModel (vote-escrow) governance locks TURBO tokens for up to 4 years for boosted rewards.
Navi Protocol supports cross-collateralization across chains via Wormhole NTT token bridge.
The Sui DeFi ecosystem's total unique users grew 10x in 2025, reaching 2M active wallets by December.
Liquidation bots on Sui earn 5-10% bonuses for liquidating undercollateralized positions on Scallop and Navi.
Sui's storage rebate system makes holding DeFi positions more gas-efficient than Ethereum.
Bucket Protocol's BUCK stablecoin maintained its $1 peg within 0.1% throughout 2025.
Mysten Labs released Move 2024 edition with improved generics and better DeFi composability patterns.
The first Sui DeFi index product launched via Aftermath Finance, tracking the top 10 Sui DeFi tokens.
DeepBook V3 introduced maker rebates and taker fees, attracting professional market makers to Sui.
Sui's programmable transaction blocks (PTBs) allow complex multi-step DeFi strategies in one atomic transaction.
Ika Protocol builds decentralized threshold ECDSA on Sui, enabling trustless cross-chain swaps.
Most Sui DeFi protocols use Sui's on-chain clock for time-based operations rather than block timestamps.
The average Sui DeFi transaction finalizes in under 500ms, enabling real-time arbitrage strategies.
Cetus V3 launched with dynamic fee tiers (0.01%, 0.05%, 0.3%, 1%) matching Uniswap V3 fee structure.
Sui's Move Prover formally verified several DeFi smart contracts for correctness in 2025.
USDT launched natively on Sui in Q2 2025, doubling stablecoin liquidity across all DEXes.
Walrus storage integration enables DeFi protocols to store trading history and analytics off-chain efficiently.
WalMarket enables trading the accumulated research memory of DeFi agents — the meta-layer above DeFi itself.
